creator: jhale@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tuesday, October 16, 2012 01:53:52 Joao Rocha Braga Filho wrote:
> On Tue, Oct 16, 2012 at 1:40 AM, Jason E. Hale <jhale@freebsd.org> wrote:
> > On Tuesday, October 16, 2012 01:11:02 Joao Rocha Braga Filho wrote:
> >> On Tue, Oct 16, 2012 at 12:45 AM, Jason E. Hale <jhale@freebsd.org> 
wrote:
> > Do you still have /usr/include/utmp.h?  If so, delete it.
> 
> Yes:
> 
> root:[178] ls -ls /usr/include/utmp.h
> 4 -r--r--r--  1 root  wheel  3045 May 18  2011 /usr/include/utmp.h
> 
> 
> make delete-old solves that?
> 
Yes.

Having that header is why x11/sessreg is not building.

> >> Man page must be updated.
> > 
> > It has been.  I don't know why yours still has ttyslot in it.
> > http://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/9/lib/libc/gen/ttyname.3?revision=22
> > 5736&view=markup
> "make delete-old" too?
> 
Maybe your documentation didn't get updated?  Running make-delete old wouldn't 
fix that.  But, that is a little off-topic for this PR.

> >> > If you upgraded this machine from FreeBSD < 9.0 at any point then you
> >> > probably have some leftovers that need to be removed which is why I
> >> > suggested running make delete-old
> >> 
> >> This system started in FreeBSD 7.x, in 02/2009, and was updated several
> >> times.
> >> 
> >> I will do "make delete-old" when FreeBSD 9.1 RELEASE. Thanks.
> > 
> > Why wait?
> 
> Now is everything working. I will update in 9.1 release, that will be soon.
> 
> >> 
> >> 
> >> And about sessreg? Will be created a patch?
> > 
> > It doesn't need a patch.  If you get rid of /usr/include/utmp.h it will
> > probably build for you.
> 
> Ok.
> 
> I used a work around. Installed the 9,0-RELEASE package.
> 
> Thanks.
